title: Alaska's Critical Mineral Engine, and Its Unbuilt AI
caption:
Alaska just won a decade-scale federal bet to find its critical minerals. The AI headlining it is one unbuilt sentence.

On July 14 the NSF named a University of Alaska Fairbanks coalition its Critical Mineral Accelerator Engine. It starts with 15 million dollars in hand, with up to 160 million over ten years if it clears milestones.

The deck covers what Alaska actually won, the 56 of 60 minerals the USGS calls essential, the money as a ladder not a check, and the single named site so far.

Funded but unspecified, is that a credible start or a headline running ahead of the work?
first_comment:
Sources for todays deck
National Science Foundation, NSF awards 12 new Regional Innovation Engines, https://www.nsf.gov/news/nsf-awards-12-new-regional-innovation-engines-fuel-research
UAF Geophysical Institute, NSF funds UAF-led critical minerals coalition, https://www.gi.alaska.edu/news/national-science-foundation-funds-uaf-led-critical-minerals-coalition
Every award we track, with the live milestones, lives at alaskaaihq.com
Close to this Engine? Add in a reply what the announcement still leaves out.
deck_summary_line: A nine-slide look at Alaska's new NSF Critical Mineral Accelerator Engine, what the 15 million dollars buys now, why the 160 million is milestone-gated, and why the AI at its center is still one unbuilt sentence.

slide_copy.overline: THE NSF JUST FUNDED AN AI TO FIND ALASKA'S CRITICAL MINERALS

slide_copy.headline: Alaska just won one of 12 new federal Innovation Engines.
slide_copy.body: NSF named a University of Alaska Fairbanks coalition its Critical Mineral Accelerator Engine. It opens with 15 million dollars for the first two years. Far more can follow, only if it delivers.

slide_copy.kicker: WHY IT IS WORTH A DECADE
slide_copy.headline: 56 of the 60 minerals the U.S. calls essential are in this ground.
slide_copy.body: That is the U.S. Geological Survey count, essential to the nation's economy and its security. Alaska is not short on critical minerals. It is short on knowing exactly where, in a place this big.

slide_copy.headline: Read the ground. Find the deposits. Pull the minerals out.
slide_copy.body: The Engine pairs advanced mining, processing and purification with two newer ideas: artificial intelligence to help locate mineral deposits, and new biological methods to extract them. This is what a prospectivity model would draw, if one existed.

slide_copy.kicker: THE ENTIRE PUBLIC DESCRIPTION OF THE AI
slide_copy.quote: use artificial intelligence to help locate mineral deposits
slide_copy.ledger: NO NAMED MODEL / NO DATASET / NO SOFTWARE / NO SITE
slide_copy.sub: It is announced and funded in principle. It is not yet specified. That is not a scandal. It is the stage this is at.

slide_copy.headline: It is a ladder, not a check.
figures: up to $160M IF MILESTONES MET over ten years
$500K ALASKA'S OWN SEED one-time state funding
slide_copy.context_strip: PROGRAM-WIDE, NOT THIS ENGINE: NSF's first $135M across nine earlier Engines drew more than $2B in matches.

slide_copy.headline: Seven minerals the announcement names.
slide_copy.swatch_labels: LITHIUM / COBALT / NICKEL / GRAPHITE / GERMANIUM / GALLIUM / RARE EARTHS
slide_copy.uses_line: Batteries, smartphones, computer chips, medical equipment, power grids, aircraft, defense. The everyday and the strategic run through the same seven materials.

slide_copy.headline: One site is named so far. The rest is still a search.
slide_copy.body: The only location named so far is Alaska Silver's exploration site in western Alaska. NANA Regional Corporation is a partner, not an owner. UAF leads it, with geoscientist Lee Ann Munk as chief executive. The goal, cut U.S. dependence on China for mineral processing.

slide_copy.statement: A real ten-year bet. An unbuilt centerpiece. Worth the climb?
slide_copy.ask: Follow the milestones, not the headline number. What would make this bet pay off for Alaska?
